sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Lille comes into this match in strong form, having won three of their last five games, including a convincing 4-0 victory over Dinamo Zagreb. Le Havre, on the other hand, is struggling, with no wins in their last three matches, though they managed a draw and a narrow win in their last five outings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two head-to-head encounters, Lille has dominated, winning 3-0 at Le Havre earlier this season, while Le Havre pulled off a surprising 2-1 victory at Lille in February 2025. Historically, Lille has the upper hand with four wins in the last five matchups against Le Havre.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Lille sits 5th in the league with 23 points and an impressive 27 goals scored, showcasing their attacking prowess. Le Havre, positioned 14th with 14 points, has struggled offensively with only 13 goals and a weaker defensive record, conceding 20 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Lille, Hakon Arnar Haraldsson and Félix Correia have been in fine form, contributing goals and assists consistently. Le Havre will rely on Issa Soumaré, who has shown promise with 3 goals and 2 assists, to spark their attack.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Lille's superior form, league standing, and head-to-head dominance, they are the clear favorites, aligning with Sofascore's 60% probability of an away win. Le Havre's home advantage and recent defensive resilience might make it competitive, but Lille's attacking depth should prevail.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Le Havre and Lille at Stade Oceane ended with a goalless draw, reflecting a tightly contested match. Neither side managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Lille dominated possession and showed more intent in terms of territory, frequently entering the final third and penalty area. However, their inability to convert opportunities into shots on target has kept the game level. Le Havre, on the other hand, made efficient use of their limited possession, registering a couple of shots on target and showing resilience in defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw frequent shifts between the two teams. Lille started strongly, controlling the early phases with sustained attacking pressure. Their dominance waned midway through the half as Le Havre responded with notable attacking spells, particularly around key moments where they pushed forward with intent. Towards the latter stages, momentum swung back and forth, with Lille regaining some initiative, but Le Havre finished the half with a flurry of activity, suggesting they could carry confidence into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Lille's performance in the first half was characterized by their high possession and frequent entries into dangerous areas, yet their lack of shots on target highlights a finishing problem. Their expected goals value suggests they had slightly better chances, and hitting the woodwork indicates they were close to scoring. Defensively, they struggled in duels, particularly in the air, which Le Havre capitalized on. Le Havre, despite lower possession, were more clinical with their limited chances, getting shots on target and showing strength in physical battles. Their high percentage of duels won, especially aerial ones, gave them a solid platform to build from.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Le Havre, players like Issa Soumaré stood out with a high performance rating, contributing both defensively and in attack. Yanis Zouaoui also made an impact with key passes, creating potential openings. Godson Kyeremeh was notable for getting a shot on target, posing a threat to Lille's defense. On Lille's side, Félix Correia showed creativity with key passes and high expected assists, while Thomas Meunier and Osame Sahraoui had decent expected goals, indicating they were in dangerous positions. Berke Özer in goal had a strong half, maintaining a clean sheet under pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Discipline and tactical outlook
halftimeSections.text: Discipline-wise, Lille picked up two yellow cards, which could affect their approach in the second half as they risk further bookings or a potential red card. Le Havre, with no cards, can afford to be more aggressive if needed. Tactically, Lille might look to sharpen their finishing and exploit their territorial advantage, possibly bringing on fresh legs to break down Le Havre's defense. Le Havre could focus on counter-attacks, utilizing their physicality and duel-winning ability to catch Lille off guard while maintaining a compact defensive shape.
